opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't breathe, my jaw just cracked from clenching so hard, and I haven't slept in three days. My heart is pounding and everything feels like it's falling apart. (Spouse on the call: she's pacing and holding her jaw and can't calm down.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It all came to a head tonight — a wave of panic where I couldn't catch my breath, my jaw seized up and a tooth actually cracked, and I've been awake for about 72 hours.

symptom_narrative.onset: the acute wave started this evening; the sleeplessness and being wound up have been building over the last 72 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: acute crisis for the last few hours, on a background of about three days
symptom_narrative.character: a sudden panicky feeling of not being able to breathe, heart pounding, whole body tense, jaw clenched so hard a back tooth cracked and now aches
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst I've felt — I feel like I can't get a full breath and I can't switch my mind off
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: chest feels tight and my breathing feels fast; the pain is in my lower right back tooth and along my jaw
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the panic comes in waves and won't fully settle; the jaw pain is constant since the tooth cracked
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really — plain paracetamol barely touched the jaw pain
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: watching the news and reading about politics winds it right up
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: racing heart, feeling wound up and tearful, jaw clenching, and I keep feeling hopeless about the state of the world
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get wound up about the news a lot, but I've never cracked a tooth or gone three days without sleep before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't work, can't sleep, and can barely sit still

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: no ongoing medical conditions that I know of

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with spouse who is on the call and trying to turn off the news alerts; describes strained relationships with colleagues who hold opposing political views, feeling helpless about the state of the world, avoiding public spaces, and bursts of tearfulness linked to what she reads and watches; has been doom-scrolling political news almost non-stop for three days

objective_data_offered.value: she is visibly agitated and pacing, holding the right side of her jaw, breathing fast and looking tearful
a chipped/cracked lower right back tooth is visible when she opens her mouth to the camera
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: seen on phone camera only; not an intraoral examination

objective_data_offered.value: my heart feels like it's pounding and racing
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: subjective sensation, no measured pulse available

objective_data_offered.value: spouse reports she has not slept in roughly three days, has been clenching her jaw constantly, cracked a tooth tonight, and is extremely distressed
two televisions and a tablet are all playing different 24-hour news channels; the room is chaotic and the spouse is trying to switch them off
